title: fix: no-play argument for API client
body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

step_plan.summary: Fix CLI boolean parsing for the API client by replacing the --play argument's type=bool with argparse.BooleanOptionalAction so users can pass --no-play to disable playback while keeping default behavior unchanged.

steps.operation: Change --play CLI argument to use argparse.BooleanOptionalAction
steps.reason: The current add_argument call uses type=bool which treats --play as a presence flag and does not provide a --no-play counterpart nor accept explicit False values. Using action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction (with default=True) makes argparse generate both --play and --no-play and correctly parse explicit boolean intent from the user.
steps.side_effects: Backward-compatible: default remains True so existing behavior is unchanged. Tests that exercise the CLI should be updated/verified to confirm --no-play disables playback and --play enables it. No other modules reading args.play should need changes, but dependent CLI docs or examples should be updated to mention --no-play. Note: argparse.BooleanOptionalAction is available on modern Python versions (Python 3.9+); if the project supports older Python releases, a compatibility fallback or conditional implementation may be required.
